what_it_does: Records metadata for a design-source image asset (deck1-2026/p-15.jpg) in the channel-memory ledger.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id, address, channel, path, blob URI, mime type, name, width/height, source, author_session, created timestamp, and empty links
The image dimensions w and h are both 0
Body text labels it as 'SOURCE design ground-truth — deck1-2026/p-15.jpg'
standouts: Width and height are 0 despite being an image ledger entry
Uses a blob://b2 URI to reference the underlying image bytes
Empty links array means no explicit relationships are declared
conventions: YAML frontmatter metadata schema for channel-memory assets
blob:// URI for binary content addressing
author_session ties the asset to a creation session
concerns: w and h are 0, suggesting missing or uncomputed image dimensions
links is empty, so no contextual relationships are captured
No alt text, caption, or content description is present
notes: This is a ledger card for a single design-source JPEG slide with placeholder dimensions and no outgoing links.
questions: Why are the image width and height recorded as 0?
What content or page in deck1-2026 does p-15 represent?
Are links intentionally empty or simply not yet populated?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record a design ground-truth source image, and the file content matches that purpose.
intention_signals: Capture and preserve a canonical, attributed pointer to a design-source image so downstream systems can locate, verify, and reference it.
novelty: Uses a blob://b2 URI to reference the underlying image bytes.
contribution: Provides a stable, attributed pointer to a design-source image asset, including a blob reference, source lineage, author session, and creation timestamp.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory ledger entry describing the design-source JPEG image deck1-2026/p-15 with metadata and a blob reference.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a design ground-truth image asset for retrieval, attribution, and provenance tracking.
